name: HRC_EVT_TYPES
description: An Event Type identifies a significant change on a data object or a particular trigger point within a process flow. It can be further qualified by Event Conditions and is used to detect the points at which subsequent processing should be invoked.

columns.Name: EVENT_TYPE_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Identifier

columns.Name: SOURCE_OBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Source Object

columns.Name: NAME
columns.Comments: Name of the event type

columns.Name: DESCRIPTION
columns.Comments: Description of the event type

columns.Name: TRIGGER_POINT
columns.Comments: Event triggering point relating to the supported trigger points with transaction based business processes

columns.Name: DML_TYPE
columns.Comments: For Entity Object and PLSQL based event types, the DML Mode that will trigger the event

columns.Name: DATE_EFFECTIVE_OPERATION
columns.Comments: For Date Effective entity object based events, the "logical" date effective operation that will trigger the event

columns.Name: GROOVY_RULE
columns.Comments: A Groovy expression that will refer to the attributes of an Entity Object

columns.Name: JAVA_RULE
columns.Comments: A Java method that can accept either an Entity Object or a Root AM, depending on the parent event type

columns.Name: PLSQL_RULE
columns.Comments: A PLSQL function that can be used to filter the PLSQL row handler based events

columns.Name: ENABLED
columns.Comments: Indicator of whether the event type is currently enabled

columns.Name: PROTECTED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Indicator that the event has been used by an application that depends on the event definition not changing in the future. It will prevent the event type from being deleted and the conditions from changing.

columns.Name: STORAGE_DURATION
columns.Comments: Number of days that the event audit data should be stored before it can be purged

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: EVENT_TYPE
columns.Comments: Discriminator to determine the event type

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
